title: Rust integer overflow panics in debug but silently wraps in release
rule: In Rust, plain +, -, * on integers are overflow-checked only when `overflow-checks` is on (default: dev=on, release=off). Debug/test builds panic ("attempt to ... with overflow"); release builds compile the check out and wrap two's-complement (255u8+1 -> 0, i32::MAX+1 -> i32::MIN). So a bug can pass debug tests yet go silently wrong in release, or pass release yet panic in CI debug. Never rely on the panic for correctness; use wrapping_/checked_/saturating_/overflowing_ explicitly, or set overflow-checks=true in the release profile.
triggers: works in release but panics in debug
passes tests but wrong result in production
code panics in CI but not locally release build
